prevalence.source: European perinatal surveillance syntheses / WHO Europe
prevalence.trend: stable to decreasing with strong infection prevention and perinatal care

burden.dalyPerCapita: high per-case; overall population burden lower due to low incidence
burden.mortalityRate: Case-fatality typically <10% in well-resourced NICUs (higher in extremely preterm infants)
burden.source: WHO Europe / IHME GBD neonatal infection estimates

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical sepsis evaluation with blood culture, inflammatory markers, and supportive diagnostics; protocols aligned with European neonatal guidelines

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: risk-based prevention (e.g., GBS screening/prophylaxis policies vary) and NICU early warning protocols
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High survival with prompt antibiotics and NICU support; ongoing focus on antimicrobial stewardship

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: High access to cultures, susceptibility testing, and NICU diagnostics
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Empiric antibiotics tailored to early vs late-onset sepsis with de-escalation based on cultures; strong supportive NICU care
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Antimicrobial resistance in some NICUs and variability in perinatal resources between member states
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Robust neonatal intensive care infrastructure and infection prevention programs in most member states
